exercises.title: A: Word formation
exercises.instructions: Use the word given in capitals at the end of each line to form a word that fits in the gap in the same line.
exercises.studentInstruction: Use the word given in capitals at the end of each line to form a word that fits in the gap in the same line.
exercises.passage:
Keeping in touch
Good (1) ...... (COMMUNICATE) is important when people live far apart. A clear (2) ...... (TRANSLATE) can help people understand messages in another language. Sometimes a word or (3) ...... (EXPRESS) sounds natural in one language but strange in another. A good (4) ...... (SPEAK) knows how to make the message sound clear. Online messages can be (5) ...... (PREDICT), so we don't always know when someone will reply. A strong Internet (6) ...... (CONNECT) makes video calls easier. I can say with (7) ...... (CERTAIN) that technology has changed friendships. People can now share (8) ...... (INFORM) instantly, write (9) ...... (SECRET) to each other, and stay in contact with friends all over the world. It has (10) ...... (CERTAIN) made communication faster.

gapExplanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is noun from COMMUNICATE, so the correct answer is "communication".

wrongOptions.communicate: The root COMMUNICATE cannot fill the gap alone — you need a derived form like "communication".

gapExplanationContent.commonMistake: Check the suffix and word class — the gap needs the form of COMMUNICATE that fits grammatically, not just the root.
gapExplanationContent.usefulTip: Identify the word class needed (noun, adjective, adverb) before choosing the suffix from the root in capitals.
gapExplanationContent.wordFormation: COMMUNICATE → communication: Use the noun form (often -tion, -ment, -ity, or -ness).
gapExplanationContent.grammarFocus: Word formation from COMMUNICATE: choose the form that matches the grammar of the line.

gapExplanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is noun from TRANSLATE, so the correct answer is "translation".

wrongOptions.translate: The root TRANSLATE cannot fill the gap alone — you need a derived form like "translation".

gapExplanationContent.commonMistake: Check the suffix and word class — the gap needs the form of TRANSLATE that fits grammatically, not just the root.
gapExplanationContent.usefulTip: Identify the word class needed (noun, adjective, adverb) before choosing the suffix from the root in capitals.
gapExplanationContent.wordFormation: TRANSLATE → translation: Use the noun form (often -tion, -ment, -ity, or -ness).
gapExplanationContent.grammarFocus: Word formation from TRANSLATE: choose the form that matches the grammar of the line.

gapExplanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is noun from EXPRESS, so the correct answer is "expression".

wrongOptions.express: The root EXPRESS cannot fill the gap alone — you need a derived form like "expression".

gapExplanationContent.commonMistake: Check the suffix and word class — the gap needs the form of EXPRESS that fits grammatically, not just the root.
gapExplanationContent.usefulTip: Identify the word class needed (noun, adjective, adverb) before choosing the suffix from the root in capitals.
gapExplanationContent.wordFormation: EXPRESS → expression: Use the noun form (often -tion, -ment, -ity, or -ness).
gapExplanationContent.grammarFocus: Word formation from EXPRESS: choose the form that matches the grammar of the line.

gapExplanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is person from SPEAK, so "speaker" fits this gap.

wrongOptions.speak: The root SPEAK cannot fill the gap alone — you need a derived form like "speaker".

gapExplanationContent.commonMistake: Check the suffix and word class — the gap needs the form of SPEAK that fits grammatically, not just the root.
gapExplanationContent.usefulTip: Identify the word class needed (noun, adjective, adverb) before choosing the suffix from the root in capitals.
gapExplanationContent.wordFormation: SPEAK → speaker: Apply the correct suffix or word form from the root.
gapExplanationContent.grammarFocus: Word formation from SPEAK: choose the form that matches the grammar of the line.

gapExplanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is adjective from PREDICT, so the correct answer is "unpredictable".

wrongOptions.predict: The root PREDICT cannot fill the gap alone — you need a derived form like "unpredictable".

gapExplanationContent.commonMistake: Check the suffix and word class — the gap needs the form of PREDICT that fits grammatically, not just the root.
gapExplanationContent.usefulTip: Identify the word class needed (noun, adjective, adverb) before choosing the suffix from the root in capitals.
gapExplanationContent.wordFormation: PREDICT → unpredictable: Use the adjective form (often -ive, -able, or a negative prefix).
gapExplanationContent.grammarFocus: Word formation from PREDICT: choose the form that matches the grammar of the line.

wrongOptions.connect: The root CONNECT cannot fill the gap alone — you need a derived form like "connection".

wrongOptions.secret: An adjective cannot modify a verb here — use the adverb form.

gapExplanationContent.commonMistake: Check the suffix and word class — the gap needs the form of SECRET that fits grammatically, not just the root.
gapExplanationContent.usefulTip: Identify the word class needed (noun, adjective, adverb) before choosing the suffix from the root in capitals.
gapExplanationContent.wordFormation: SECRET → secretly: Add the adverb suffix (-ly) or use the adverb form.
gapExplanationContent.grammarFocus: Word formation from SECRET: choose the form that matches the grammar of the line.

gapExplanationContent.wordFormation: CERTAIN → certainly: Add the adverb suffix (-ly) or use the adverb form.
gapExplanationContent.grammarFocus: Word formation from CERTAIN: choose the form that matches the grammar of the line.

exercises.exerciseTypeName: B: Write one word in each gap
exercises.title: B: Write one word in each gap
exercises.instructions: Write one word in each gap.
exercises.studentInstruction: Write one word in each gap.

items.sentence: When does the next issue of that science magazine come ...... ?

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is be published, so the correct answer is "out".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Compare your answer with the grammar or vocabulary clue in the sentence — tense, form, or collocation may be wrong.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Relative clauses add detail about a noun — choose the pronoun that matches the antecedent.

items.sentence: The storm damaged the line and cut the Internet ...... .

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is disconnect, so the correct answer is "off".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Check whether the noun is specific, countable, or mentioned for the first time.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Articles show whether a noun is specific (the), new and countable (a/an), or general.

items.sentence: I'm busy now, but I'll call you ...... after lunch.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is return a call, so the correct answer is "back".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Compare your answer with the grammar or vocabulary clue in the sentence — tense, form, or collocation may be wrong.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.vocabularyFocus: "back" is the only word or phrase that fits both grammar and meaning in: "I'm busy now, but I'll call you ...... after lunch.".

items.sentence: He got angry and hung ...... before I could explain.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is end a call, so the correct answer is "up".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Compare your answer with the grammar or vocabulary clue in the sentence — tense, form, or collocation may be wrong.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.vocabularyFocus: "up" is the only word or phrase that fits both grammar and meaning in: "He got angry and hung ...... before I could explain.".

items.sentence: Can you print this booking confirmation ...... for me?

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is make a copy, so the correct answer is "out".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Try saying the phrase aloud — the correct preposition is usually part of a fixed pattern.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Prepositions often form fixed patterns — learn them as chunks, not word by word.

items.sentence: She logged ...... the school website to check her homework.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is sign in, so the correct answer is "onto".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Check whether the noun is specific, countable, or mentioned for the first time.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Articles show whether a noun is specific (the), new and countable (a/an), or general.

items.sentence: Please fill ...... your name and address clearly.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is complete a form, so the correct answer is "in".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Try saying the phrase aloud — the correct preposition is usually part of a fixed pattern.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Prepositions often form fixed patterns — learn them as chunks, not word by word.

items.sentence: After checking my messages, I logged ...... and shut down the computer.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is sign out, so the correct answer is "off".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Compare your answer with the grammar or vocabulary clue in the sentence — tense, form, or collocation may be wrong.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.vocabularyFocus: "off" is the only word or phrase that fits both grammar and meaning in: "After checking my messages, I logged ...... and shut down the computer.".

exercises.title: C: Key Word Transformation
exercises.instructions: Complete each second sentence using the word given, so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence. Write between two and five words.
exercises.studentInstruction: Complete each second sentence using the word given, so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence. Write between two and five words.

items.sentence:
Laura sent me an invitation by e-mail. **received**
I ...... Laura.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is passive receive, so the correct answer is "received an invitation from".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Check the word order and particles — English often needs the full chunk "received an invitation from", not just one word.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Passive voice focuses on the action or object, not who performs it.

items.sentence:
It isn't necessary to bring your own headphones. **have**
You ...... bring your own headphones.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is not necessary, so the correct answer is "don't have to".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Check the word order and particles — English often needs the full chunk "don't have to", not just one word.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Modals express advice, obligation, permission, or prohibition — choose the nuance that matches the sentence.

items.sentence:
Nina is able to speak and understand German. **can**
Nina ...... German.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is ability, so the correct answer is "can speak and understand".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Check the word order and particles — English often needs the full chunk "can speak and understand", not just one word.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Prepositions often form fixed patterns — learn them as chunks, not word by word.

items.sentence:
It wasn't necessary for us to print the tickets. **need**
We ...... to print the tickets.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is not necessary (past), so the correct answer is "didn't need".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Check the word order and particles — English often needs the full chunk "didn't need", not just one word.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Prepositions often form fixed patterns — learn them as chunks, not word by word.

items.sentence:
It's possible that the message is from our teacher. **might**
The message ...... from our teacher.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is possibility, so the correct answer is "might be".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Check the word order and particles — English often needs the full chunk "might be", not just one word.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Relative clauses add detail about a noun — choose the pronoun that matches the antecedent.

exercises.title: E: Complete the sentences
exercises.instructions: Write one word in each gap.
exercises.studentInstruction: Write one word in each gap.

items.sentence: I need to ...... a message to the club secretary.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is send a message, so the correct answer is "send".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Compare your answer with the grammar or vocabulary clue in the sentence — tense, form, or collocation may be wrong.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.vocabularyFocus: "send" is the only word or phrase that fits both grammar and meaning in: "I need to ...... a message to the club secretary.".

items.sentence: She ...... the envelope carefully before posting the letter.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is close an envelope, so the correct answer is "sealed".
explanationContent.commonMistake: Check whether the noun is specific, countable, or mentioned for the first time.
explanationContent.usefulTip: The words before the arrow in the original note are your clue — match that meaning to the gap.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Articles show whether a noun is specific (the), new and countable (a/an), or general.

explanationContent.vocabularyFocus: "take" is the only word or phrase that fits both grammar and meaning in: "Could you ...... this parcel to the front desk?".

items.sentence: I ...... him a voice message, but he hasn't listened to it yet.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The clue is past of send, so the correct answer is "sent".

explanationContent.grammarFocus: Present perfect links a past action to the present or unfinished time.
